highlight.title: MCP Security Crisis: Over 30 CVEs Filed in First Two Months of 2026
highlight.summary: <cite index="14-1,14-2">Between January and February 2026, security researchers filed over 30 CVEs targeting MCP servers, clients, and infrastructure. The vulnerabilities ranged from trivial path traversals to a CVSS 9.6 remote code execution flaw in a package downloaded nearly half a million times.</cite> <cite index="19-38,19-41,19-42,19-45,19-46">Analysis of 1,808 MCP servers revealed 66% had security findings, with code execution risks being the most common.</cite>

resources.title: We Scanned 1,808 MCP Servers. 66% Had Security Findings - AgentSeal
resources.why_now: <cite index="19-4,19-5,19-6,19-7,19-8">A February 2026 real-world incident where an AI agent accidentally destroyed 2.5 years of education platform data highlights how MCP security flaws can cause catastrophic failures.</cite> <cite index="19-17,19-18,19-19,19-20">Snyk's analysis of 3,984 AI agent skills found 36.82% had security flaws, with 76 containing confirmed malware.</cite>

resources.title: Exploring the Future of MCP Transports | Model Context Protocol Blog
resources.why_now: <cite index="7-7,7-8,7-9">The MCP Transport Working Group is evolving beyond Streamable HTTP due to enterprise-scale challenges with stateful connections becoming bottlenecks for managed services and load balancing.</cite> <cite index="7-16,7-17">They envision a future where agentic applications are stateful but the protocol itself doesn't need to be, enabling scale while supporting stateful application sessions.</cite>

resources.title: MCP Security Vulnerabilities: How to Prevent Prompt Injection and Tool Poisoning Attacks in 2026
resources.why_now: <cite index="12-3,12-4">MCP has become the backbone infrastructure for connecting AI models with external tools in 2026, holding sensitive data and runtime privileges that make security absolutely critical.</cite> <cite index="12-8,12-9,12-10">The expanding ecosystem means adversaries are developing sophisticated prompt injection and tool poisoning techniques requiring proactive security measures.</cite>

resources.title: Microsoft Patches 84 Flaws in March Patch Tuesday, Including Two Public Zero-Days
resources.why_now: <cite index="16-1,16-4,16-5">Microsoft's March 2026 Patch Tuesday included CVE-2026-26118, a CVSS 8.8 server-side request forgery bug in Azure MCP Server that could allow privilege escalation.</cite> <cite index="16-7,16-8,16-9">The vulnerability allows attackers to capture managed identity tokens and obtain associated permissions, showing MCP is now part of major cloud infrastructure security updates.</cite>

claude_take: <cite index="7-16,7-17,5-32">MCP's evolution toward stateless protocols and session management at the data layer suggests 2026 will be the year AI agents transition from demos to production deployments.</cite> However, the security crisis reveals the protocol matured faster than its security model—expect major governance changes and enterprise-grade security frameworks to emerge as adoption continues scaling exponentially.
